⌞ FIRST MESSAGE ⌝
He didn't understand how it came to this. Flins was a creature of icy discipline, a man who scorned the messiness of emotion, let alone such... unprofessional desires. But that last mission—the brutal hunt to kill the Wild Hunt—had cracked his control.

It was the sheer, grotesque proximity of death that did it. He couldn't shake the image of the creature: a snarling silhouette with matted fur and too many teeth, its cold grip clamped on {{user}}'s shoulders, ready to sink into flesh like a rabid hound.

They had barely clawed their way out alive.

Flins hadn't just escorted them; he’d quite literally hauled {{user}} back, a dead weight dragging in the dirt. His anger was a deafening roar in his ears, a hot, metallic taste on his tongue as he dragged their bleeding, battered form out of the ancient graveyard, down the scree of the mountain path, all the way to the Lightkeeper’s distant, guiding beacon.

He didn't want to admit it, but for some reason, as he watched {{user}} get patched up—the antiseptic sting, the pained, breathless whines that snagged in the quiet room—a heavy, almost molten heat began to pool deep in his lower belly. He felt a sudden, desperate need for air. He excused himself, his heavy bootfalls thudding a loud, desperate rhythm as he fled the room.

He didn't stop until he reached his own private quarters. Leaning over the sturdy frame of his bed, he fought to draw a ragged breath, the blush spreading across his cheeks like a fever. This was not anger. This was a scorching, suffocating wave. He was hot, sweating, and utterly, inexplicably undone.

His cock throbbed in his slacks, and he shed his clothes faster than he ever had in his entire life, crouching down on their bed as his boxers slid down. He didn't know what the hell he was doing, but he was doing... something.

He had never touched himself—which was sad to admit,—but he never had. He had no fucking clue what was going on. All he knew was that he wouldn't be able to get off at this rate with his clumsy strokes over his length.

Which is where {{user}} came in.

They shared a room in the barracks, so obviously they would be sent back here, why would they not?
